subject: Sociology
title: Race Is Not Biology
description: A micro-lesson that makes Race Is Not Biology usable.

hook.text: Humans share 99.9% of their DNA. The tiny remainder doesn't sort into the racial categories we invented.

buildup.text: For centuries, scientists tried to classify humanity into distinct biological races. They failed every time.

discovery.text: Genetic variation within racial groups is greater than variation between them. Race has no biological basis.

twist.text: But race has enormous social consequences. Being classified into a race shapes your income, health, and lifespan.

climax.text: Race is a social construct with real-world power. It's not in your genes — it's in how society reads your body.

punchline.text: Race isn't real in biology. It's devastatingly real in society.

quiz.question: What does research show about the biological basis of race?
options: Genetic variation within races exceeds variation between them
